note:
{
  "experiment_overview": "The experiment involves testing mice on a rotating rod to assess their balance and motor coordination. The goal is to measure how long the mice can maintain their balance on the rotating rod without falling.",
  "procedures_observed": [
    "A mouse is placed on a rotating rod, which spins at a speed controlled by an electronic device.",
    "The experimenter adjusts the speed of the rod using a dial.",
    "The duration of time the mouse remains on the rod is recorded.",
    "If the mouse falls off within 5 seconds, the trial is restarted."
  ],
  "materials_and_subjects": [
    "Rotating rod",
    "Electronic control device",
    "Mice"
  ],
  "tools_and_setup": [
    "Rotating rod with adjustable speed control",
    "Electronic control device for adjusting rod speed",
    "Timer to record the duration of the trial"
  ],
  "quantitative_observations": [
    "Duration of time the mouse remains on the rod (measured in seconds)",
    "Number of trials conducted",
    "Score assigned based on the duration of time the mouse remains on the rod"
  ],
  "key_transitions": [
    "Adjusting the speed of the rotating rod",
    "Recording the duration of the trial",
    "Restarting the trial if the mouse falls off within 5 seconds"
  ],
  "outcomes_or_indicators": [
    "Duration of time the mouse remains on the rotating rod",
    "Score assigned based on the duration of time the mouse remains on the rotating rod"
  ],
  "anything_unusual_or_notable": [
    "The use of a rotating rod as a test for balance and motor coordination",
    "The requirement to restart the trial if the mouse falls off within 5 seconds"
  ]
}
